message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step reasoning per main node:

1) REG-092 (Art77(1))
- Requirement: Right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority if the data subject considers processing infringes “this Regulation,” especially in the Member State of habitual residence, place of work, or alleged infringement. No explicit cross-references in the clause.
- Policy text: “In accordance with Art 77 GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.”
- Analysis:
  - The policy correctly acknowledges the existence of the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
  - It omits locations (habitual residence, place of work, place of alleged infringement). Per instruction, omissions are treated as non-deviations.
  - It states the condition as “infringes data protection law,” which is broader than “infringes this Regulation (GDPR).” This broadens the scope of when the right can be exercised beyond GDPR to other data protection laws. This is a difference in scope and should be classified as a Data deviation (scope expansion). No cross-references apply.

2) REG-093 (Art77(2))
- Requirement: The supervisory authority with which the complaint is lodged must inform the complainant about progress and outcome, including the possibility of a judicial remedy pursuant to Article 78.
- Cross-references considered: Article 78’s judicial remedies (REG-094 Art78(1), REG-095 Art78(2), REG-096 Art78(3), REG-097 Art78(4)).
- Policy text: Only mentions the right to lodge a complaint (Art 77). It does not address the SA’s duty to inform or the judicial remedy information.
- Analysis:
  - The policy chunk is silent on the SA’s information duties and on judicial remedies under Article 78. Per the instructions, missing content is treated as a non-deviation (omission). No contradictory or narrowing statements are present.

3) REG-095 (Art78(2))
- Requirement: Effective judicial remedy if the competent supervisory authority (per Arts 55 and 56) does not handle a complaint or fails to inform the data subject within three months about progress or outcome of the complaint lodged under Art 77.
- Cross-references considered: Article 77 (REG-092 and REG-093) because 78(2) hinges on a complaint lodged pursuant to 77. (Articles 55 and 56 are referenced in the clause but not provided in referenced_nodes; analysis focuses on available nodes.)
- Policy text: Only mentions the existence of the Art 77 complaint right; no mention of the three-month period, handling failures, or judicial remedy.
- Analysis:
  - The policy chunk does not discuss judicial remedies or timelines; per instructions, this omission is a Non-Deviation. No contradictory timelines or statements are present.

Conclusion:
- REG-092: One Data deviation due to replacing “infringes this Regulation” with broader “infringes data protection law.”
- REG-093: No deviation (omission only).
- REG-095: No deviation (omission only).
</deliberation>

  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "analysis": "Art77(1) grants a right to lodge a complaint if processing infringes \"this Regulation\" (GDPR). The policy states the right applies if processing infringes \"data protection law,\" which broadens the trigger from GDPR to any data protection law. While not necessarily non-compliant, this is a scope difference. Location specifics (habitual residence, place of work, or alleged infringement) and the \"without prejudice\" qualifier are omitted, which per instructions are treated as omissions, not deviations."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",

  }
